content.claim: Villages with primary schools do not show clearly better socioeconomic conditions overall—they demonstrate better demographic performance and partly better living/infrastructural conditions, but worse employment and welfare characteristics compared to villages without schools
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Authors explicitly flag this finding as ambiguous and contested
Causal direction unclear—schools may follow or precede community advantage
Correlations vary by dimension measured (demographics, employment, welfare), preventing unified conclusion
Cross-national comparison (Hungary-Romania) may not isolate Hungary-specific factors
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
